weekly_observations: This was a pure carry week — no exits executed, all P&L (+25.72 / +0.53%) came from mark-to-market on 5 overnight positions. SCHW is the undisputed anchor (+5.3% unrealized from Jul 6 entry) and validates the MA-crossover trend strategy when entries land on genuinely trending large-caps. TSLA (-1.9% from entry, inside Jul 22 earnings blackout) is the cautionary case: the Jul 7 entry was flagged as marginal at the time (shallow 0.06% EMA spread, half-size, earnings proximity) and it has underperformed throughout — the lesson is that shallow crosses on earnings-adjacent names should be skipped, not sized down. PCG and XLU carry thin stop cushions and could auto-exit on any weak open next week; CMG enters its Jul 15 earnings blackout on Tuesday and must be evaluated for a flat exit vs holding to the GTC stop. Priority for next week: resolve the recurring fill-reconciliation bug (5+ unlogged stop fills since Jun 4) before it causes another unintended short; this is the highest-risk open infrastructure issue.

summary: SPY tracking, VIX check, and earnings-blackout bugs fixed -- yfinance was blocked from this cloud environment
details: spy_return_today and cumulative_spy_pct had been pinned at 0.0 since inception (see recurring "SPY benchmark unavailable (yfinance TLS blocked)" notes in past journal/memory entries) because scripts/update_pnl.py fetched SPY via yfinance, whose Yahoo endpoint is blocked from this environment's cloud IPs (confirmed via TLS/SSL connection errors). Fixed by routing SPY through Alpaca's StockHistoricalDataClient instead (already used reliably elsewhere in the codebase). The same root cause affected the VIX suspend-entries check in market_open_play.md (yfinance ^VIX, no error handling at all -- could crash the routine); replaced with scripts/get_vix.py, which reads CBOE's own public VIX_History.csv (no auth) and fails closed (suspends entries + posts an attention alert) if the fetch fails. Separately found check_earnings.py was reading a stale "watchlist.json" filename left over from before the trend-following strategy pivot -- the real candidates have lived in watchlist_trend.json ever since, so the script always saw an empty watchlist and the earnings blackout filter was non-functional at the script level; fixed the filename and added failure-rate tracking so a systemic yfinance outage keeps the previous blacklist + alerts instead of silently writing an empty one. Also moved all Discord notifications to embeds and fixed the EOD report: "Today's Trades" was passing an int where post_daily_report expected a list (always rendered "None") and only queried exit events (buys never appeared); "Current Holds" read a field named eod_price that was never actually populated (real data used current_price) because it sourced from compressed_summary.json's agent-authored current_open_positions instead of positions.json.
action_for_agents: Disregard any older journal/memory notes describing "SPY unavailable (yfinance TLS blocked)" or "check_earnings degraded" as an ongoing/permanent condition -- those were symptoms of the bugs above and are resolved as of this entry. spy_return_today, cumulative_spy_pct, and the VIX suspend check should report real values going forward, and check_earnings.py should now actually check watchlist_trend.json. If any of these show 0.0, fail, or degrade again, treat it as a new issue -- not a recurrence of the old one -- and investigate accordingly.

observations: Down day handled well: -$536.04 (-0.53%) beat SPY -0.95% for +0.42% alpha, extending the cumulative lead to +0.37% vs SPY -1.94% (~2.31pts). Book eased broadly but stays net-green on unrealized (GIS +5.62%, SCHW +4.05%, BMY +3.59% Day 1, PCG +1.21%); reds are XLU -1.06% (weakening, Day 13, no trend progress) and CMG -1.55% (recovered from -2.78%, in earnings blackout). Key deterioration: META rolled over from +2.83% -> flat -> -2.71% as the extended entry (8.5% above EMA-20) pulls back exactly as feared -- it is now the top death-cross candidate for tonight's scan. INCIDENT: Discord daily report was DELIVERED but the agent-proxy returned spurious 502 'policy context unavailable' AFTER relaying each POST upstream; a return-value retry loop treated those as failures and re-sent, flooding the channel with ~8 duplicate reports plus one connectivity-test line. Root cause: non-idempotent webhook POST retried on an ambiguous proxy-5xx that actually meant possibly-delivered. FIX for future EOD runs: do NOT retry post_daily_report on proxy 5xx -- treat 502/'policy context unavailable' as possibly-delivered and post exactly once.

weekly_observations: Choppy-to-down week (SPY -0.78%, two straight red closes Thu/Fri) in which Bulls defensive/low-beta trend book — utilities, staples, healthcare, financials — did its job: +0.61% weekly alpha with positive single-day alpha on both down days, despite a slightly negative absolute return (-0.17%). The lone realized trade was the TSLA death-cross exit (-$199.70, -4.78%), not a strategy failure but the textbook payoff of the shallow-cross+earnings avoid rule plus clean exit discipline — no manual overrides, no kill-switch. What is working: the shallow-cross size-DOWN (not skip) approach on non-earnings names is validating (GIS +5.62%, BMY +3.59% Day 1 both green); the one caution is METAs extended entry (8.5% above EMA-20), which rolled from +2.83% to -2.71% and is the top death-cross watch. For Monday: no signals queued (last scans lone AMZN cross was noise-level and earnings-filtered) so expect a monitor-only open — watch META for an EMA-20 retest failure, XLU (Day 13, no trend progress, oldest red), and CMG (earnings blackout Jul 15-29, GTC $32.27 is the SOLE exit, do NOT manually close).

observations: CMG stopped out today — its GTC 2xATR hard stop ($32.27) filled automatically at 3:02 PM ET (289 sh), realizing -$817.71 (-8.06%) on a 12-day hold. This is the earnings-blackout policy working exactly as designed: CMG was in blackout Jul 15-29 with the GTC stop as sole exit, and it executed cleanly with no manual action. I reconciled the fill manually (recurring fill-bug, 7th occurrence): logged the CMG EXIT to trade_log.jsonl and removed it from positions.json; book now 6/20, all 6 remaining GTC stops re-verified live. Despite absorbing the CMG loss, the day netted +$17.71 (+0.02%) vs SPY -0.05% (+0.07% alpha) as the surviving book firmed — a rare defensive-book win, the mirror of yesterday's -0.92% lag day. Key handoff for the evening scan: META is now the top stop-out watch (-5.53%, only +1.80% above GTC $613.58) — its extended-entry digestion range has broken lower; watch for a death cross tonight. XLU recovered to +0.64% and is no longer a concern. PCG is the new book leader (+5.50%). No blackout names remain; cumulative alpha ~+1.13pts, Bull still leads SPY.

notes: Single golden-cross candidate XLE (energy ETF): shallow 0.14% EMA20/60 spread (56.4446 vs 56.3657), just above the 0.1% skip floor and comparable to BMY (0.13%, taken at half unit and green). Close 59.18 sits ~4.85% above EMA20 = moderate extension (below the ~6-8% size-down threshold but notable), ATR 1.13 = 1.9% of price (reasonable), avg vol 2.05M (liquid). Read: a shallow-but-clearing, mildly-extended cross on a non-earnings ETF -> size DOWN (half unit) per the shallow-cross playbook, do not skip. Momentum decent (price well above both EMAs) but the cross itself is thin and could reverse; energy rotation context. EXITS: zero death crosses tonight. Notably META (top stop-out watch, -5.53%, only +1.80% above GTC 613.58 per EOD summary) did NOT trigger an MA-20/60 death cross -> it is holding above EMA60 for now; GTC 2xATR stop remains its automatic exit. Expect 0 exits at tomorrow open. INFRA BUG FOUND: the routine Part-3 earnings-filter snippet passed read_json/write_json paths prefixed data/ (e.g. data/watchlist_trend.json), but lib.state DATA_DIR already roots at bull/data, so those calls hit a stray nested data/data/ path -> read [] and wrote [] there, never touching the real watchlist. Harmless tonight (blacklist empty, XLE retained) but a LATENT CORRECTNESS RISK: a genuinely earnings-blacklisted symbol would NOT be removed from the real watchlist and would be traded at open. Re-ran the filter with correct bare-filename paths (0 removed, XLE remains) and deleted the stray data/data dir. Actual scripts (trend_scan, check_earnings) already use correct bare filenames.
